serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Chicken Char Siew Flaky Pastry at a dim sum restaurant or bakery is 3 pieces, weighing approximately 150 g in total.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pork Char Siu Bao (Baked BBQ Pork Pastry) and Pastry, Puff, Frozen, Ready-to-Bake, Baked; adapted for chicken char siew flaky pastry using HPB Singapore nutrient composition data for similar dim sum baked pastry items
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Health Promotion Board, Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for chicken char siew flaky pastry. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entries for baked BBQ pork pastry (char siu bao baked) and puff pastry, cross-referenced with HPB Singapore nutrient composition data for similar baked dim sum snacks. The input record values (energy 320 kcal, protein 12 g, fat 20 g, carbohydrate 23.33 g per 100 g) are consistent with this adapted composite and are retained. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, and water data are not available in the adapted source combination and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Pastry, flaky/puff type, wheat flour based, analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for chicken char siew flaky pastry. GI estimated at 70 (high) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for wheat-flour-based flaky/puff pastry products, which report GI values in the 65–75 range. The refined wheat flour pastry shell is the dominant carbohydrate contributor. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (70 × 35.0) / 100 = 24.5, where carbohydrate per 150 g serving = 23.33 × 1.5 = 35.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 433 mg/100 g from soy sauce and oyster sauce in filling, combined with high saturated fat (10 g/100 g) and low potassium, with no meaningful fibre offset
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit intake to one piece or avoid entirely; the sodium load from soy and oyster sauce in the char siew filling (433 mg/100 g) combined with butter-derived saturated fat (10 g/100 g) promotes both fluid retention and arterial stiffness, and the low potassium and fibre content provide no compensatory offset.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI refined wheat flour pastry (estimated GI 70) with 23.3 g carbohydrate/100 g, 6.7 g added sugar/100 g from honey and glaze, and only 1.3 g dietary fibre/100 g
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this item; the refined wheat flour shell carries an estimated GI of 70 and delivers 35 g of rapidly absorbed carbohydrate per standard 3-piece serving, while the honey and sugar glaze (6.7 g sugar/100 g) and minimal fibre (1.3 g/100 g) further accelerate postprandial glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 10 g/100 g from butter-based pastry, trans fat 0.33 g/100 g, cholesterol 50 mg/100 g, and sodium 433 mg/100 g collectively elevate LDL-C and blood pressure risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease should avoid this item; the butter-based flaky pastry delivers 10 g saturated fat and 0.33 g trans fat per 100 g, which raise LDL cholesterol and promote atherogenesis, while the sodium content (433 mg/100 g; 650 mg per 150 g serving) adds meaningful blood pressure burden.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat 10 g/100 g from butter (double the 5 g/100 g RA threshold) promotes TNF-alpha and IL-6 pro-inflammatory cytokine production
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this item; the butter-based pastry shell contributes 10 g saturated fat per 100 g — double the RA threshold — which promotes TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory cytokines, while the honey and sugar glaze (6.7 g/100 g) further contributes to systemic inflammatory mediator production.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Char Siew Flaky Pastry (鸡叉烧酥; Ji Cha Shao Su) is a baked dim sum pastry popular in Singapore and across Chinese-Singaporean bakeries and yum cha restaurants. The outer shell is a golden, multi-layered flaky pastry made from wheat flour and butter using a laminated or water-oil dough technique, producing a crisp, shatteringly flaky texture. The filling consists of diced or minced chicken marinated and cooked in a char siew (BBQ) style with soy sauce, oyster sauce, honey, sugar, sesame oil, and five-spice powder, yielding a sweet-savoury, slightly sticky filling with caramelised edges. The pastry is brushed with egg wash before baking to achieve a deep golden glaze. Per 100 g, it provides approximately 320 kcal, 12 g protein, 20 g fat (of which 10 g is saturated fat from butter), 23.3 g carbohydrate, 6.7 g sugar, 1.3 g dietary fibre, and 433 mg sodium. The high saturated fat from butter, elevated sodium from soy and oyster sauces, and refined-flour high-GI carbohydrate profile make this item a high-concern food for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. It is typically served as a snack or dim sum item in portions of 3 pieces (~150 g). Common allergens include gluten, egg, dairy, soy, and sesame.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for chicken char siew flaky pastry exists in USDA FoodData Central or the HPB Singapore database. Nutrient values are adapted from the closest analogous USDA entries for baked BBQ pork pastry and puff pastry, cross-referenced with HPB Singapore data for similar baked dim sum snacks. The input record values are consistent with this adapted composite and are retained. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for wheat-flour flaky pastry. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this specific chicken char siew flaky pastry preparation is not available in USDA FoodData Central or the HPB Singapore database as of 2024. 2. potassium_mg is set to -1: potassium data for this composite dish is not reported in the adapted source combination. 3. water_g is set to -1: moisture content for this specific baked pastry preparation is not available in the adapted sources. 4. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are set to -1: micronutrient data beyond calcium, iron, and vitamin C is not available in the adapted source combination for this dish. 5. vitamin_c_mg is reported as 0.0 (confirmed zero): baked pastry with cooked chicken filling contains negligible vitamin C; this is consistent with USDA data for similar baked meat-filled pastry products. 6. trans_fat_g of 0.33 g/100 g is retained from the input record; this is plausible for a butter-based flaky pastry and is consistent with USDA data for similar products. 7. Halal status is marked false as char siew traditionally uses pork-derived sauces and the halal certification of the specific preparation is unknown; chicken char siew may be halal-certified at specific outlets but this cannot be confirmed generically. 8. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for wheat-flour-based flaky/puff pastry; no direct GI measurement for this specific dish was found.
